mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in a t-test against baseline returns.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and capital investment, which directly boosts corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when both series grow, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in multivariate regression over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing and utility output, which signals immediate economic momentum and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 5-day returns in >55% of cases, or the predictive power vanishes during inflationary regimes (CPIAUCSL > 3.5% YoY).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0%, rising inflation measured by CPIAUCSL forces aggressive monetary tightening expectations, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in >60% of instances where both conditions are met, or the interaction effect reverses sign during disinflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding employment and rising aggregate wages, which sustains corporate profit growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns in >58% of observations, or Rank IC drops below 0.01 across all rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 1.5% annually signals weakening aggregate demand and contracting corporate earnings, which directly produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of low-growth quarters, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with stable or falling unemployment indicates healthy capacity utilization without labor market stress, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to predict positive 5-day returns in >60% of cases, or the signal becomes negative when unemployment rises alongside production.
